LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT NASER ZAKOUT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAY LEAVE TO AMEND, MR. ZAKOUT'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL PERTAINS TO A BINDING PROMISE MADE WITHOUT CONSIDERATION. (FONTENOT V. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 256, 275.) IN THIS CASE, MR. IKFAFI ALLEGES THAT THAT A WRITTEN AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO WHERE CONSIDERATION WAS BARGAINED FOR, AND THUS THE DOCTRINE OF PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL IS INAPPLICABLE. LEAVE IS GRANTED IF MR. IKFAFI CAN PLEAD IN GOOD FAITH A PROMISE MADE BY MR. ZAKOUT WITHOUT CONSIDERATION. THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. WHETHER MR. IKFAFI WAS A PARTNER OR EMPLOYEE IS A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT SUITABLE ON A DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BRYAN MCCORMACK, ESQ., 415.296.8420, FOR PLAINTIFF OMAR IKFAFI.
S. CHRISTINE YOUNG, ESQ., 415.217.8802, FOR DEFENDANT NASER ZAKOUT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT NASER ZAKOUT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained, with 10 day leave to amend, Mr. Zakout's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for promissory estoppel, and overruled as to the remaining causes of action. For the fourth cause of action, promissory estoppel pertains to a binding promise made without consideration. (Fontenot v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 256, 275.) In this case, Mr. Ikfafi alleges that that a written agreement was entered into where consideration was bargained for, and thus the doctrine of promissory estoppel is inapplicable. Leave is granted if Mr. Ikfafi can plead in good faith a promise made by Mr. Zakout without consideration. The remaining causes of action are adequately pled. Whether Mr. Ikfafi was a partner or employee is a question of fact that is not suitable on a demurrer. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
